name: Magic Item Crafting and Sentient Items
description: Rules for crafting magic items, tool requirements, time/cost, and creating sentient magic items with personality and conflict mechanics

tools.title: Tools
tools.overview: The Magic Item Tools table lists which tool is required to make a magic item of each category
tools.requirement: You must use the required tool to make an item and have proficiency with that tool. Any assistants must also have proficiency with it

rows.required_tool: Leatherworker's Tools, Smith's Tools, or Weaver's Tools depending on the kind of armor as noted in the tools' descriptions
Leatherworker's Tools, Smith's Tools, or Woodcarver's Tools depending on the kind of weapon as noted in the tools' descriptions
Tinker's Tools or the tool required to make the nonmagical item on which the magic item is based

spells.title: Spells
spells.description: If a magic item allows its user to cast any spells from it, you must have all those spells prepared every day you spend crafting the item

time_and_cost.title: Time and Cost
time_and_cost.overview: Crafting a magic item takes an amount of time and money based on the item's rarity

table.title: Magic Item Crafting Time and Cost
notes: The time and cost are halved for a consumable item other than a Spell Scroll
Spell Scroll crafting time and cost are given in Equipment

work_per_day.title: Work per Day
work_per_day.description: For each day of crafting, you must work for 8 hours
work_per_day.flexibility: If an item requires multiple days, those days needn't be consecutive

assistants.title: Assistants
assistants.description: Characters can combine their efforts to shorten the crafting time
assistants.calculation: Divide the time needed to create an item by the number of characters working on it
assistants.limitation: Normally, only one other character can assist you, but the DM might allow more assistants

raw_materials.title: Raw Materials
raw_materials.description: The cost in the table represents the raw materials needed to make a magic item
raw_materials.availability: The DM determines whether appropriate raw materials are available
raw_materials.urban_availability: In a city, there is a 75 percent chance that the materials are available
raw_materials.settlement_availability: In any other settlement, that chance is 25 percent
raw_materials.unavailable: If materials aren't available, you must wait at least 7 days before checking on the availability again

base_item_cost.description: If a magic item incorporates an item that has a purchase cost (such as a weapon or a suit of armor), you must also pay that entire cost or craft that item using the rules in Equipment
base_item_cost.example: To make +1 Armor (Plate Armor), you must pay 3,500 GP or pay 2,000 GP and craft the armor

sentient_magic_items.title: Sentient Magic Items
sentient_magic_items.overview: Some magic items have sentience and personality
origins: Items haunted by the spirit of a previous owner
Items self-aware thanks to the magic used to create them
sentient_magic_items.relationship: A sentient item might be a cherished ally to its wielder or a continual thorn in the side
sentient_magic_items.prevalence: Most sentient items are weapons, but other kinds of items can manifest sentience
sentient_magic_items.limitation: Single-use items such as potions and scrolls are never sentient
sentient_magic_items.control: The DM controls sentient magic items and their activated properties
access: A bearer who maintains a good relationship with the item can access those properties
If the relationship is strained, a conflict can ensue

sentient_magic_item_traits.title: Sentient Magic Item Traits
sentient_magic_item_traits.overview: When you make a sentient magic item, you create the item's persona much as you would create an NPC, with these exceptions

abilities.title: Abilities
abilities.description: A sentient magic item has Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores
abilities.selection: Choose the item's abilities, or determine them randomly
abilities.random_method: Roll 4d6 for each one, dropping the lowest roll and totaling the rest

alignment.title: Alignment
alignment.description: A sentient magic item has an alignment
alignment.determination: Its creator or nature might suggest an alignment. Otherwise, pick an alignment or roll on the Sentient Item's Alignment table

communication.title: Communication
communication.description: A sentient item communicates by sharing its emotions, broadcasting its thoughts telepathically, or speaking aloud
communication.choice: You can choose how it communicates or roll on the Sentient Item's Communication table

senses.title: Senses
senses.description: A sentient item can perceive its surroundings out to a limited range
senses.choice: You can choose its senses or roll on the Sentient Item's Senses table

special_purpose.title: Special Purpose
special_purpose.description: You can give a sentient item an objective it pursues, perhaps to the exclusion of all else
special_purpose.alignment_condition: As long as the wielder's use of the item aligns with that special purpose, the item remains cooperative
special_purpose.deviation: Deviating from this course might cause conflict between the wielder and the item
special_purpose.choice: You can pick a special purpose or roll on the Sentient Item's Special Purpose table

rolls.communication: The item communicates by transmitting emotion to the creature carrying or wielding it
The item speaks one or more languages
The item speaks one or more languages. In addition, the item can communicate telepathically with any creature that carries or wields it

rolls.senses: Hearing and standard vision out to 30 feet
Hearing and standard vision out to 60 feet
Hearing and standard vision out to 120 feet
Hearing and Darkvision out to 120 feet

rolls.description: The item seeks to defeat or destroy those of a diametrically opposed alignment. Such an item is never Neutral
The item seeks to thwart or destroy creatures of a particular type, such as Constructs, Fiends, or Undead
The item seeks its creator and wants to understand why it was created
The item believes it and its bearer have key roles to play in future events
The item craves destruction and goads its user to fight arbitrarily
The item seeks renown as the greatest magic item in the world by winning fame or notoriety for its user
The item craves knowledge or is determined to solve a mystery, learn a secret, or unravel a cryptic prophecy
The item seeks to defend a particular kind of creature, such as elves or werewolves
The item seeks another sentient magic item, perhaps one that is similar to itself
The item seeks to defend the servants and interests of a particular deity

conflict.title: Conflict
conflict.overview: When the bearer of a sentient item acts in a manner opposed to the item's alignment or purpose, conflict can arise
conflict.trigger: When such a conflict occurs, the item's bearer makes a Charisma saving throw (DC 12 plus the item's Charisma modifier)

demands_on_failure.description: On a failed save, the item makes one or more of the following demands

demands.effect: The item demands that its bearer pursue the item's goals to the exclusion of all other goals
The item demands that its bearer dispose of anything the item finds repugnant
The item demands to be given to someone else
The item insists on being carried or worn at all times

refusal_consequences.description: If its bearer refuses to comply with the item's demands, the item can do any of the following

consequences.effect: Make it impossible for its bearer to attune to it
Suppress one or more of its activated properties

consequences.trigger: Attempt to take control of its bearer
consequences.mechanism: The bearer makes a Charisma saving throw (DC 12 plus the item's Charisma modifier)

failure.effect: The bearer has the Charmed condition for 1d12 hours
failure.compliance: While Charmed in this way, the bearer must try to follow the item's commands

consequences.damage_escape: If the bearer takes damage, it repeats the save, ending the effect on a success
consequences.cooldown: Whether or not the attempt to control its bearer succeeds, the item can't use this power again until the next dawn

ability_scores.method: 4d6 drop lowest for Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma

conflict_mechanics.damage_escape: Damage allows repeat save to end Charmed

contains: Magic Item Tools table (9 categories with specific tool requirements)
Tool proficiency requirements for crafter and assistants
Spell preparation requirement for spell-casting items
Magic Item Crafting Time and Cost table (Common to Legendary)
Work per day rules (8 hours minimum per day)
Assistants and time division mechanics
Raw materials availability (75% city, 25% settlement)
Material recheck timing (7 days minimum)
Base item cost addition for weapons and armor
Sentient magic items overview (origins, relationship, limitations)
Sentient item trait creation (Abilities, Alignment, Communication, Senses, Special Purpose)
Ability score determination (4d6 drop lowest)
Sentient Item's Alignment table (9 alignments, 01-00 roll)
Sentient Item's Communication table (3 options, 1d10 roll)
Sentient Item's Senses table (4 options, 1d4 roll)
Sentient Item's Special Purpose table (10 purposes, 1d10 roll)
Conflict trigger and Charisma saving throw (DC 12 + CHA mod)
Four conflict demands (Chase Dreams, Get Rid of It, Change Bearer, Keep Me Close)
Refusal consequences (attunement revocation, property suppression, control attempt)
Control attempt mechanics (Charmed condition, 1d12 hours, damage escape)
Control cooldown (resets at next dawn)
